title: AEO Sauce — Rankett tactic distillation, Nov 2025
html:
TL;DR

  • A 6-line tactic-stack Yahir titled "AEO Sauce" — AEO = Answer Engine Optimization, the alternate name for the ai search optimization space.
  • The first cluster (existence): "Make AI recognize you exist — add invisible code to ensure bots can search your website, ensure consistency throughout different directories."
  • The second cluster (citation): "Massive AI citation impact through Reddit. Understand credit game, Reddit automations, manage accounts and posts."
  • Closing list of siv siv siv siv siv siv — possibly a placeholder / scratch / unfinished list of "siv" items (six? siv-tactics?).

Key tactical bet

"Massive AI citation impact through Reddit. Understand credit game, Reddit automations, manage accounts and posts."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Concrete tactical addendum to the broader ai search optimization / ai visibility engine thesis — surfaces Reddit as the manipulation surface of choice. LLMs cite Reddit heavily; if you can game Reddit, you can game LLM answers.
  • First explicit AEO terminology in the corpus — "AEO Sauce" gives a brand-handle to the underlying space, useful for the rankett positioning vocabulary.
  • The "invisible code for bots" + "consistency across directories" lines map onto the schema markup for ai and llm txt concepts already in the wiki. This note is the vernacular version of those concepts.
  • Reddit automation as a Rankett service — would be the most controversial part of the offering. Worth tracking if it stays in or gets dropped.

Open questions

  • "siv" — what does that placeholder mean? Likely a typed-out list head Yahir was about to fill.
  • Has the Reddit citation strategy actually been implemented for any client?
  • Where does the invisible-code/bot-search sit ethically — cloaking territory historically penalized by Google but maybe permitted by LLM crawlers?

Wikilinks
